explanation: This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the STANDBY state and the pilot is not in control (i.e., !standby), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode and the pilot is not in control (i.e., !standby), the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step.  This is expressed using the next-time operator (X) and the implication operator (->) within the always operator (G).

The LTL property ensures that whenever the autopilot is in the STANDBY state and the pilot is not in control (not standby), the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the STANDBY state and the pilot is not in control, it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within a duration of N_DURATION time steps. This property ensures that the autopilot will respond to the pilot's request to take control by transitioning to the appropriate state within a reasonable time frame.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in the STANDBY state and the pilot is not in control, the autopilot must transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. It uses the atomic proposition "state_is_STANDBY" to indicate the autopilot is in the STANDBY state, "!standby" to indicate the pilot is not in control (not standby), and "state_is_TRANSITION" to indicate the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state. The next operator (X) ensures that the transition happens in the following time step.
